title: Androrchis × fitzii
taxonomy: Androrchis × fitzii is a hybrid of the orchid genus Androrchis. It is a natural hybrid between Androrchis hircattica and Androrchis palustris. The classification of Androrchis × fitzii is currently recognized in botanical literature as a distinct species within the Orchidaceae family. This classification is based on its unique characteristics and genetic makeup. Further research and studies may provide more insights into the classification and evolution of this orchid species.

intro: Androrchis × fitzii, also known as Fitz's orchid, is a hybrid orchid species that has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Androrchis × fitzii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Androrchis × fitzii is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
